name: Ruidera — circular from Castillo de Peñarroya
name_original: Ruidera — circular desde el Castillo de Peñarroya
region_intro: No traffic touring cycling routes in Lagunas De Ruidera traverse a unique hydrological system of 15 interconnected lagoons, stretching over 25 kilometers. The landscape is characterized by crystal-clear, turquoise waters that spill from one lagoon to the next over natural tufa barriers, contrasting with the surrounding arid plains. The area features diverse vegetation, including reeds near the water's edge and juniper and holm oak trees at higher elevations. Best no traffic touring cycling routes in Lagunas De Ruidera The most popular no traffic touring cycling route is Lagunas de Ruidera Loop, a 17.4 miles (27.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 15 minutes to complete. This route offers panoramic views of the interconnected lagoons and their tufa formations. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Laguna del Rey – Lagunas de Ruidera loop from Ruidera, a difficult 29.2 miles (47.0 km) path. This route involves significant elevation changes and passes by historic sites. Local touring cyclists also love the Access to La Isla – Lagunas de Ruidera loop from Ruidera, a 11.0 miles (17.7 km) trail leading through the diverse lagoon system, often completed in about 1 hour. Touring cycling in Lagunas De Ruidera is defined by interconnected lagoons, natural tufa barriers, and juniper and holm oak forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Lagunas De Ruidera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Lagunas De Ruidera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many no traffic touring cycling routes are available in Lagunas De Ruidera?
A: There are &lt;em&gt;over 25&lt;/em&gt; dedicated no traffic touring cycling routes in Lagunas De Ruidera, offering a variety of experiences. These routes allow you to explore the unique hydrological system and diverse landscapes without vehicle interference.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about the no traffic routes in Lagunas De Ruidera?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from 28 ratings. Reviewers often praise the stunning natural beauty of the interconnected lagoons, the crystal-clear waters, and the peaceful, car-free environment that allows for immersive cycling.

Q: Are there easy no traffic touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Lagunas De Ruidera?
A: Yes, Lagunas De Ruidera offers 5 easy no traffic touring cycling routes perfect for beginners or those seeking a relaxed ride. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40346151" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Access to La Isla – Lagunas de Ruidera loop from Ruidera&lt;/a&gt;, which is 17.7 km long with minimal elevation gain.

Q: Can I find challenging car-free touring cycling routes in Lagunas De Ruidera?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists looking for a challenge, there are 16 difficult no traffic touring cycling routes. Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063931056" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagunas de Ruidera Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 27.9 km route with significant elevation changes, offering panoramic views of the lagoons and tufa formations.

Q: Are there loop touring bike routes without traffic in Lagunas De Ruidera?
A: Many of the no traffic touring cycling routes in Lagunas De Ruidera are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6901305" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Rey – Lagunas de Ruidera loop from Ruidera&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 47 km route that takes you through diverse parts of the lagoon system.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the no traffic touring cycling routes?
A: The routes wind through the heart of the natural park, offering views of the 15 interconnected lagoons, their crystal-clear turquoise waters, and spectacular waterfalls like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3844258" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Batanas Lagoon Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. You'll also encounter diverse vegetation, from reeds at the water's edge to juniper and holm oak trees at higher elevations. Don't miss the vibrant &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/322233" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagoons in the most beautiful colors&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there historical landmarks or cultural sites near the car-free cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history and culture. You can cycle past significant sites such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2719681" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñarroya Castle&lt;/a&gt;, a historic fortress with Muslim origins, and the ruins of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4085693" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rochafrida Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The area is also deeply connected to Cervantes' "Don Quixote de la Mancha," with the famous Cueva de Montesinos nearby.

Q: What is the best season for car-free touring cycling in Lagunas De Ruidera?
A: The best seasons for car-free touring cycling in Lagunas De Ruidera are spring and autumn. During these times, the weather is mild, the landscapes are lush, and the crowds are generally smaller, providing ideal conditions for enjoying the natural beauty and quiet routes.

Q: Is there parking available near the no traffic touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, there are several designated parking areas throughout the Lagunas de Ruidera Natural Park, particularly near popular access points to the lagoons and villages like Ruidera. These spots often provide convenient starting points for the no traffic cycling routes.

Q: Are the no traffic touring cycling routes in Lagunas De Ruidera suitable for families?
A: Many of the easier no traffic touring cycling routes are suitable for families, especially those with older children. The flat sections along the lagoons offer a safe and enjoyable environment. Always check the route difficulty and length to ensure it matches your family's capabilities. The park also features designated bathing areas in several lagoons, perfect for a family break.

Q: Are there places to swim near the car-free cycling routes?
A: Yes, several lagoons within the park have designated bathing areas where you can take a refreshing dip. Popular spots include Laguna del Rey, Colgada, Santos Morcillo, Salvadora, Redondilla, and San Pedro. These are often easily accessible from the cycling routes.
tips: Ruidera is a Spanish municipality and town in the province of Ciudad Real, in the autonomous community of Castilla-La Mancha. Located in the Campo de Montiel region, it is known for its lagoons, which constitute the headwaters of the Guadiana River.

The King's Lagoon, one of the most emblematic lagoons in the Ruidera Lakes Natural Park:
It is located within the municipality of Ruidera (Ciudad Real), at the main entrance to the natural park.

It is one of the first lagoons you see when traveling through the park by road, very close to the town of Ruidera.
It stands out for the turquoise color of its waters, which in some places are reminiscent of the Caribbean.
It is photogenic and easily accessible, ideal for those looking for a place to relax or take a swim.
The bottom is clearly visible thanks to the water's transparency.

The Peñarroya Reservoir, like many others across Spain, was built during the Franco dictatorship. Its main purpose is to supply drinking water to nearby towns and irrigate the fields of La Mancha. It is also well-known among fishing enthusiasts who flock to the reservoir's shores every weekend in search of a photograph-worthy catch.
In summer, the Peñarroya Reservoir is the second most popular bathing destination within the Lagunas de Ruidera Natural Park.

The fortress originated as a Muslim fort, which would pass into Christian hands in 1198, remaining in the power of the Order of San Juan in 1215, although it was conquered with the help of the Order of Santiago. Legend has it that after taking the fortress, the image of Our Lady of Peñarroya was found, venerated since then by the residents of Argamasilla de Alba and La Solana, who share the Patron Saint.
